serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of chicken and waffles consisting of one or two pieces of crispy fried chicken placed on a Belgian waffle, drizzled with maple syrup and accompanied by a small side salad, weighing approximately 380 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken and waffles composite dish — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, meat only, cooked, fried (FDC ID 171477); Waffles, plain, prepared from recipe (FDC ID 172033); Syrups, maple (FDC ID 169661); Salad greens, raw (FDC ID 169247)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for chicken-and-waffles as a composite dish. Nutrient values per 100 g were estimated by weighted combination of the four component foods using representative serving proportions: fried chicken breast 160 g, Belgian waffle 130 g, maple syrup 30 g, mixed salad greens 60 g (total 380 g). Weighted per-100 g values were derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods entries for each component.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — waffle / pancake analogous estimate; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021;114(5):1625-1632.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chicken-and-waffles as a composite dish. GI of 62 is estimated using a weighted-carbohydrate approach: Belgian waffle GI ~76 (Atkinson et al. 2021, waffle entry), maple syrup GI ~54 (Atkinson et al. 2021), fried chicken breast GI ~0 (protein/fat dominant, negligible glycaemic contribution). Weighted GI = (waffle carb contribution × 76 + syrup carb contribution × 54) / total carbohydrate per serving. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (62 × 104.1) / 100 ≈ 64.5, rounded to 64.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g; a full serving (~380 g) delivers approximately 1178 mg sodium, representing roughly 51% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and request reduced-sodium preparation, as the combined sodium from the fried chicken coating, waffle batter, and any added condiments can contribute substantially to daily sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (~64) driven by refined-flour waffle and maple syrup; a single serving delivers approximately 104 g of carbohydrate with a medium-to-high glycaemic impact.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat chicken and waffles as an occasional indulgence, consider halving the waffle portion, substituting maple syrup with a low-GI alternative, and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 2.9 g per 100 g (approximately 11 g per serving) from deep-frying oil, waffle butter, and chicken skin; combined with moderate cholesterol (~62 mg per 100 g) and high sodium.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption, opt for air-fried or baked chicken variants where available, and be mindful that the saturated fat and sodium content of a full serving may approach or exceed recommended single-meal limits for cardiovascular risk management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken and Waffles is a Western comfort-food dish originating in the United States, popularised in soul-food cuisine and now widely served in cafes and brunch restaurants across Singapore and globally. The dish consists of crispy deep-fried or pan-fried chicken — typically a breast or thigh piece coated in seasoned batter — placed directly on top of a freshly cooked Belgian-style waffle. The combination is finished with a generous drizzle of maple syrup and often accompanied by a small side salad to balance the richness. The flavour profile is a deliberate sweet-savoury contrast: the crunchy, salty, umami-rich fried chicken plays against the soft, slightly sweet, buttery waffle and the floral sweetness of maple syrup. Texturally, the dish offers crunch from the chicken crust, a pillowy interior from the waffle, and a light freshness from the salad. Nutritionally, a standard restaurant serving (~380 g) is energy-dense at approximately 942 kcal, providing around 50 g of protein, 37 g of fat, and over 100 g of carbohydrate. The glycemic load per serving is high (~64), driven primarily by the refined-flour waffle and maple syrup. Sodium content is moderate to high (~1178 mg per serving), largely from the seasoned chicken coating. The dish is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease without portion modification. In Singapore, it is commonly found on cafe and brunch menus under names such as 'Chic & Waffles' or 'Crispy Chicken Waffles', and is classified as a dessert or brunch item depending on the establishment.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chicken and waffles?
Can diabetics eat chicken and waffles?
What is the GI of chicken and waffles?
How much sodium in chicken and waffles?
What allergens are in chicken and waffles?
Where can I get chicken and waffles in Singapore?
What is Chic and Waffles at The Tree Cafe?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for chicken-and-waffles as a composite dish in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods, or any other primary regional database. Nutrient values were estimated by weighted combination of four USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries using representative restaurant-portion proportions. GI was estimated using a weighted-carbohydrate method anchored to approved GI database values for waffle and maple syrup components. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for fried chicken breast (FDC 171477), plain waffle from recipe (FDC 172033), maple syrup (FDC 169661), and mixed salad greens (FDC 169247). No direct composite-dish entry was available in any approved primary source. 2. Serving size of 380 g is an estimate based on typical Singapore cafe plating; actual restaurant portions may vary. 3. Halal status is marked false because the dish as served at The Tree Cafe — Funan has not been confirmed as MUIS-certified halal; consumers should verify directly with the restaurant. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, and all B-vitamins and minerals are estimated from USDA component data and carry higher uncertainty than the core macros. 5. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of the accessed date. 6. The food_name has been normalised from the promotional menu name 'Chic & Waffles ⭐' to the canonical English name 'Chicken and Waffles'; the original menu name is preserved in aliases. 7. Multilingual names are provided under Singaporean-dish rules because this dish is served in Singapore, even though it originates in the United States. 8. All -1 sentinel values in per_100g have been replaced with estimated numeric values derived from USDA component data; no -1 values remain in this record.
